assumptions: Please act as a professional verilog designer.

Implement a Multiplying Accumulator for 32bit integer. In the MAC_PE, there is a register that stores the partial sum (the intermediate accumulation result), and in each cycle, the result of “a multiplied by b” will be accumulated in this register, and the “c” shows the value of the register.
The module implements a parallel multiplier and accumulator using registers and an always block. It multiplies the input operands and accumulates the result into an output register.
The module includes a register, c, to store the accumulated result.
On the positive edge of the clock signal (clk) or the positive edge of the reset signal (rst), the module updates the register.

If the reset signal (rst) is high, indicating a reset condition, the register (c) is set to 0. If the reset signal (rst) is low, the register (c) is updated by adding the product of the input operands (a and b) to its current value.

Inside the always block, the module performs the multiplication and accumulation operation. If the reset signal (rst) is high, the register (c) is set to 0. If the reset signal (rst) is low, the module adds the product of the input operands (a and b) to the register (c).
Task description was provided and only used as a low-risk planning hint.
Spec text was provided and only used where it did not contradict the contract.

expected_properties: Observed outputs or protocol-visible signals settle to a stable, non-X post-reset state.
No fixed-cycle completion is assumed during reset recovery.
cases.goal: Establish a stable post-reset baseline before functional checking.
notes: Reset polarity may still be heuristic if the contract marks it ambiguous.
preconditions: Reset signal is available in the contract.
stimulus_intent: Assert the detected reset using the inferred polarity.
Release reset conservatively and observe interface stabilization.
timing_assumptions: Do not assume completion before it becomes externally visible.

expected_properties: A legal operation causes an observable state or output change after conservative observation.
The plan does not assume a completion event before it is externally visible.
c updates by a*b on the next observable clock edge when rst is low.
notes: Case intent is conservative when the contract is weak or timing is unresolved.
Ensures basic functional correctness without assuming hidden latency.
preconditions: Use only ports and observable signals present in the contract.
stimulus_intent: Drive a representative legal input pattern across known inputs: ['clk', 'rst', 'a', 'b']
Apply a non-zero a and b while rst is low and capture c.
timing_assumptions: Do not assume completion before it becomes externally visible.
Observe c after one rising edge of clk.

When width expressions are symbolic, only conservative min/max-style exploration is planned.

notes: Edge coverage remains value-oriented and avoids fixed-latency assumptions.

timing_assumptions: Do not assume completion before it becomes externally visible.

expected_properties: Accumulator wraps on 32-bit overflow consistent with Verilog arithmetic semantics.
cases.goal: Verify behavior when multiplication result exceeds 32-bit width and accumulates, ensuring defined wrap-around.
notes: Product is 64-bit but only lower 32 bits are stored in c per Verilog truncation.
stimulus_intent: Apply maximum operand values to provoke overflow.
timing_assumptions: Observe c after one clock edge following stimulus.

expected_properties: c increments by product each cycle, cumulative sum observable.
cases.goal: Demonstrate correct accumulation over several consecutive cycles.
notes: Uses a sequence of known operand pairs.
stimulus_intent: Drive a sequence of (a,b) pairs across successive clock cycles.
timing_assumptions: Conservative observation after each rising edge.
